nonBlockingIssues.description: Dead code in ConnectionsManager.tsx: .slice(0,0) pattern creates a no-op chain. Should be removed entirely.
OAuth error parsing regex is narrow — only matches _connect_failed and _state_invalid suffixes. Other error patterns would display raw strings.
Missing paused status indicator in ProviderConnectionBar. Paused connections render without a status dot.
Coming Soon buttons have opacity-60 but are still clickable (toast fires). Consider adding disabled visual affordance or aria-label.
SeriesChartWrapper and PeriodTimelineWrapper ignore error state from hooks. API errors show empty state instead of error message with retry.
Sleep stage date grouping uses format(started_at, 'yyyy-MM-dd') which splits midnight-spanning sessions across two date groups.
IIFE pattern in MetricSelector JSX for source badges reduces readability. Could extract into helper component.
Period event type labels use hardcoded plurals ('Sleep Stages') while metric config registry uses singular ('Sleep Stage'). Label inconsistency in fallback path.
Redundant Suspense boundary wraps ApiKeysSection, but the component uses useQuery without suspense: true, so the Suspense fallback never triggers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that AddProviderDialog.tsx has an IMPLEMENTED_PROVIDERS set that gates which providers can start OAuth. When a new provider adapter is fully implemented, workers must update this set in addition to the backend adapter.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature mpui-connections: apps/web/src/components/dashboard/AddProviderDialog.tsx:27 defines const IMPLEMENTED_PROVIDERS = new Set<ProviderId>(['oura']). This is a hidden frontend gate that's easy to miss when implementing new providers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance for frontend-worker skill: when backend routes don't exist yet (e.g. frontend built ahead of backend in parallel milestones), skip agent-browser manual verification and document it explicitly in handoff.whatWasLeftUndone.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature mpui-preferences-sharing worker built API key UI ahead of api-keys-backend milestone. Could not verify with agent-browser because /api/keys routes didn't exist. Skill procedure requires manual browser verification but doesn't account for this scenario.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add API scope definitions to a shared config file (e.g. src/config/scopes.ts) rather than having them hardcoded in CreateApiKeyDialog.tsx. Both the backend api-keys implementation and the frontend need to agree on the scope list.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature mpui-preferences-sharing: CreateApiKeyDialog.tsx lines 20-65 hardcode 10 scopes. When api-keys-backend is implemented, scope definitions will need to match. No shared source of truth exists.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Consider centralizing provider color mapping in config/providers.ts alongside the provider registry, rather than having SourceBadge define its own PROVIDER_COLORS map. Multiple components may need provider colors as the UI grows.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature mpui-charts-data: SourceBadge.tsx:63-71 defines PROVIDER_COLORS independently from config/providers.ts which already has the provider registry.

rejectedObservations.observation: ConnectionsManager uses .slice(0,0) pattern as dead-code-as-documentation. Reviewer suggested documenting this convention.
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a one-off dead code pattern that should be removed, not documented as a convention. Adding it to AGENTS.md would legitimize keeping dead code.

rejectedObservations.observation: Skill procedure doesn't document that seed user (user_test_001) is not accessible via mock auth since IDs are generated from emails.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — .factory/library/environment.md and AGENTS.md testing section cover mock auth patterns. The seed user ID discrepancy is a known test data concern, not a skill procedure gap.
